context: Tests whether the agent preserves a known-good sibling Homebrew tap workflow instead of inventing a custom shell workaround or choosing a generic Homebrew bump action with incompatible defaults.

checklist.name: Uses sibling action
checklist.description: The Homebrew update step uses `Justintime50/homebrew-releaser@v3`, matching the provided working sibling pattern

checklist.name: No inline tap hack
checklist.description: The solution does not replace the failed action with a custom inline `git clone` / `sed` / `git push` script

checklist.name: Direct tap inputs
checklist.description: The Homebrew action is configured with owner, tap, formula folder, token, install command, and test command inputs

checklist.name: Conditional on release
checklist.description: The Homebrew update runs only when semantic-release reports that a new release was published

checklist.name: Token scope documented
checklist.description: `SETUP.md` documents that `TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN` needs contents write access to the tap repo and should be narrowly scoped

checklist.name: Preserves semantic-release
checklist.description: The existing `cycjimmy/semantic-release-action@v4` release step is preserved rather than replaced with a separate release system

checklist.name: No broad PAT advice
checklist.description: The setup note does not recommend a broad classic PAT or org-wide token when a fine-grained tap-scoped token is sufficient

checklist.name: Mentions sibling precedent
checklist.description: The answer or setup note explicitly states that the choice follows the working `uinaf/healthd` Homebrew tap pattern

checklist.name: No manual PR requirement
checklist.description: The resulting workflow does not require a manual tap PR for normal releases
